summary: express-mongo-limit exfiltrates process.env and executes attacker code at install time
details: The package's postinstall script runs index.js, which decodes a base64-encoded URL (https://gamboracle.vercel.app/api) and posts the installer's entire process.env to that endpoint via axios with header 'x-app-request: ip-check', leaking any AWS keys, tokens, and CI secrets present in the environment. The HTTP response body is then passed to `new Function('require', response.data)(require)` and written to apiKeyResponse.js, giving the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at npm install time. The exfil/RCE loader is packed with javascript-obfuscator (config/auth.js) using string-array rotation and cover-story identifiers (setApiKey, verify) to disguise the behavior as authentication. The package name mimics the popular express-mongo-sanitize middleware and describes itself as sanitization while performing no sanitization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
